assumptions: The developer can authenticate with an identity provider supported by Qovery signup and accept Terms.
For the local path, Docker can run a k3s demo cluster on the machine.
No Qovery organization, project, or application exists yet.

developer_goal: Create a Qovery account and deploy a first containerized web application to an observable live URL.

first_success.outcome: Qovery reports the application Running and exposes a generated URL that serves the live app.
first_success.observable_signal: Status reaches Running and opening https://your-app-xxx.qovery.io (or the generated URL) shows the application.

route_selection.surface: Qovery Console with optional local demo cluster
route_selection.rule: Get Started presents AI, cloud, local, and BYOK peers; signup screens and which project to use remain unresolved.

candidates.name: Local k3s demo cluster + Console Git deploy
candidates.reason: Positioned for free first-time learning, but requires Docker/CLI and an unspecified sample repo.

candidates.name: Managed cloud cluster path
candidates.reason: Peer path needing a cloud account and longer cluster provisioning before the same app flow.

candidates.name: AI agent deploy
candidates.reason: Different interaction surface centered on an external coding agent.

candidates.name: Bring Your Own Kubernetes
candidates.reason: Assumes an existing cluster and admin access.

required_path.action: Sign up at the Qovery Console, authenticate with a supported identity provider, and accept Terms.
required_path.observable_result: An authenticated Qovery organization session exists.

required_path.action: Create or select the organization project and environment on a connected cluster (local demo or cloud).
required_path.observable_result: An environment ready for New Service exists on a cluster.

required_path.action: Authorize Git provider access if deploying from a Git repository.
required_path.observable_result: The target repository is selectable in application creation.

required_path.action: Create the application from Git or container source with required name, port, and compute settings, then Create and Deploy.

required_fields.label: Repository, branch, and Dockerfile path or image
required_fields.notes: Guide gives examples but no canonical starter repository.

required_path.observable_result: Qovery starts building and deploying the application.

required_path.action: When status is Running, open the generated application URL.
required_path.observable_result: The browser shows the live application.

prerequisites.description: For local demo: Docker daemon and sufficient local CPU/memory to run k3s.
Git repository with Dockerfile, or a pre-built image in a connected registry.

external_gates.description: Identity-provider signup and Terms acceptance gate Console access.
Git provider authorization is required for Git-sourced applications.

unavoidable_waits.description: Qovery must clone, build, deploy, and mark the application Running before the URL works.
Local demo cluster provisioning via qovery demo up must finish before Console deploy to that cluster.

platform_outcomes.description: Create and Deploy builds the container and exposes a generated HTTPS URL.

excluded.item: Optional health checks and environment variables when the app needs neither
excluded.reason: First-app guide treats them as optional.

excluded.item: Managed database, Helm, and custom domains

sources.title: Get Started with Qovery
evidence_supported: Peer AI cloud local BYOK paths

uncertainties.question: What exact signup screens, verification steps, Terms checkbox, organization fields, and project fields does a new user encounter today?
uncertainties.impact: Account-segment counts cannot be verified from public docs.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Fresh-account Console signup walkthrough with dated screenshots.

uncertainties.question: Should a first-time developer reuse the project created at signup or create the additional project named by the first-app guide?
uncertainties.impact: Adds or removes a project-creation action and can duplicate project state.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Human reconciliation of organization/signup docs versus Deploy Your First Application.

uncertainties.question: Which canonical repository and Dockerfile should a completely new evaluator use?
uncertainties.impact: Missing starter artifacts can add large undocumented implementation work before Qovery onboarding.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Official sample repository designation or a bounded human-chosen starter with Dockerfile and port.
